title: Uganda Family Safety Protocol (DEMO - Historical)
description: Family safety measures during state-sponsored violence. Amin's regime killed an estimated 100,000-500,000 people. Disappearances were common.
items: Establish code words for danger - phone lines were monitored by State Research Bureau
Multiple family members should NOT travel together initially - reduces risk of entire family being detained or killed
Women and children evacuate first if possible - men faced higher risk of detention, torture, or execution
Keep low profile - no displays of wealth, no political discussions, no criticism of government even in private
Memorize emergency contacts - written lists could be used as evidence against you
Establish meeting point in destination country - families were often separated during hasty evacuations
Curfew compliance - violators were shot on sight without warning. 9pm-6am curfew strictly enforced.
Avoid checkpoints if possible - soldiers were unpaid, predatory, and often drunk or high. Extortion, assault, and murder were common.
Carry small amounts of cash for bribes - but not too much, which makes you a target
Know location of safe houses - trusted community members or religious institutions offered temporary refuge
Children should know: where to go, who to trust, and basic survival information in case of family separation
Register with High Commission or Embassy if nationality unclear - though protection was limited, it created paper trail
